source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Multiple agents in same workspace
notes: One opencode session and at least one different-adapter session driven concurrently in the SAME cwd by run-cell-multi.sh, observed by a single --record daemon. opencode lives in a ProcessOwnedStore (SQLite ~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db) keyed by directory; the partner adapters live in disjoint FilesUnderRoot/FilesUnderCWD transports, so the daemon labels each adapter independently and the workspace events.jsonl carries distinct sessions — one per adapter — each with its own working->ready arc and no cross-contamination. This spec is adapter-agnostic: agentA is whichever session enters working first, agentB the other; this opencode cell stores the opencode transcript for the replay golden and shares the workspace events.jsonl with the other cross_adapter cells. Every phase anchors on a WORKING transition, never a ready, because the process scanner emits a pre-session proc-<PID> row born in ready that never works — anchoring on working skips those placeholders, and new_session on agentB skips agentA's already-matched session. opencode's default model is a slow local LM Studio model (~30-45s/turn), so agentA's first-to-working may be a faster partner agent and opencode may be agentB; the spec is symmetric and validates either ordering. Asserts: two distinct sessions, each an independent working->ready arc, in one workspace.

text: First agent's turn drives ready -> working (binds agentA; skips both adapters' born-ready proc-<PID> placeholders by anchoring on working). Wide window absorbs opencode's slow local model.
First agent's turn completes: working -> ready
The OTHER agent (a DISTINCT session, different adapter, same cwd) drives ready -> working independently (new_session proves it is not agentA; working anchor skips the placeholders)
Second agent's turn completes: working -> ready — two agents, one workspace, labeled independently with no cross-contamination
